segments.text: Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; preserve my life from dread of the enemy.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked, from the throng of evildoers,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: who whet their tongues like swords, who aim bitter words like arrows,
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: shooting from ambush at the blameless, shooting at him suddenly and without fear.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: They hold fast to their evil purpose; they talk of laying snares secretly, thinking, 'Who can see them?'
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: of…: Heb. to hide his snares

segments.text: They search out injustice, saying, 'We have accomplished a diligent search.' For the inward thought and the heart of a man are deep.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: they…: or, we are consumed by that which they have throughly searched
a diligent…: Heb. a search searched

segments.text: But God shall shoot his arrow at them; they shall be wounded suddenly.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: shall they…: Heb. their wound shall be

segments.text: They are brought to ruin, with their own tongues turned against them; all who see them will wag their heads.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then all mankind fears; they tell what God has brought about and ponder his work.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let the righteous one rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him! Let all the upright in heart exult!
segments.speaker: David
